2603.20919.title: Enhancing LIME using Neural Decision Trees
2603.20919.paper_summary: Interpreting complex machine learning models is a critical challenge, especially for tabular data where model transparency is paramount. Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) has been a very popular framework for interpretable machine learning, also inspiring many extensions. While traditional surrogate models used in LIME variants (e.g. linear regression and decision trees) offer a degree of stability, they can struggle to faithfully capture the complex non-linear decision boundaries that are inherent in many sophisticated black-box models. This work contributes toward bridging the gap between high predictive performance and interpretable decision-making. Specifically, we propose the NDT-LIME variant that integrates Neural Decision Trees (NDTs) as surrogate models. By leveraging the structured, hierarchical nature of NDTs, our approach aims at providing more accurate and meaningful local explanations. We evaluate its effectiveness on several benchmark tabular datasets, showing consistent improvements in explanation fidelity over traditional LIME surrogates.
2603.20919.paper_summary_zh: 解釋複雜的機器學習模型是一項關鍵挑戰，尤其是對於表格數據而言，模型透明度至關重要。局部可解釋模型無關解釋（LIME）是一個非常受歡迎的可解釋機器學習框架，也啟發了許多擴展。雖然在 LIME 變體中使用的傳統替代模型（例如線性回歸和決策樹）提供了一定程度的穩定性，但它們在忠實捕捉許多複雜黑箱模型固有的非線性決策邊界方面可能會遇到困難。本研究致力於縮小高預測性能與可解釋決策之間的差距。具體而言，我們提出了 NDT-LIME 變體，將神經決策樹（NDTs）作為替代模型進行整合。通過利用 NDTs 的結構化和層次化特性，我們的方法旨在提供更準確和有意義的局部解釋。我們在幾個基準表格數據集上評估其有效性，顯示出相較於傳統 LIME 替代模型在解釋真實性方面的一致性提升。
2603.20919.author: Mohamed Aymen Bouyahia et.al.
